description: US-03 Know Which Group Collects Today — Red/Blue week colour derived from the calendar by strict alternation from a configured anchor, with a prominent banner and any-week lookup.

userStories.title: weekColour calendar alternation rule
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a pure function from a date to a week colour, so the Red/Blue alternation is testable without a database. Source: tasks/prd-us-03-week-colour.md §US-03.1
acceptanceCriteria: src/domain/distribution/weekColour.ts exports colourOf(date, anchor) where anchor is {isoWeek, colour}
The colour flips on every ISO week boundary; parity is computed from the ISO week difference to the anchor
colourOf is total for dates before the anchor — tests cover one, two and fifty-three weeks before it
Property test asserts colourOf(d) !== colourOf(d + 7 days) for a swept range of dates
Tests cover ISO edge cases: the Sunday-to-Monday boundary, 1 January falling in week 52/53 of the prior ISO year, and a 53-week year
ISO-8601 week numbering (weeks start Monday) is used and documented in a comment
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: colourOf + isoWeekOf in src/domain/distribution/weekColour.ts; 23 tests including a five-year alternation sweep. The anchor is re-validated here (2025-W53 is well-formed but does not exist).

userStories.title: isDistributionDay and nextDistribution
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want the app to know whether today is a distribution day and, if not, when the next one is and what colour it will be. Source: tasks/prd-us-03-week-colour.md §US-03.2
acceptanceCriteria: isDistributionDay(date, weekday) compares against the configured ISO weekday
nextDistribution(date, settings) returns {date, colour} for the next distribution day at or after date
On a distribution day, nextDistribution returns today, not next week
Tests cover the day before, the day of and the day after a distribution day, and a configured weekday of Sunday (ISO 7)
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: isDistributionDay + nextDistribution in src/domain/distribution/distributionDay.ts; 13 tests. weekColour.ts now exports startOfUtcDay and isoWeekdayOf so both modules share one piece of calendar arithmetic.

userStories.title: getWeekColour use case
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a use case that resolves the settings in force and returns the colour for any date. Source: tasks/prd-us-03-week-colour.md §US-03.3
acceptanceCriteria: getWeekColour(deps, date?) resolves settings at that date, then delegates to the domain function
Defaults to deps.clock.now() when no date is given
Test proves a lookup for a past date uses the anchor that was in force then, not the current anchor
No week rows are ever persisted — the use case needs only SettingsRepository
Tested against hand-written fakes and a fake clock
Tests pass
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: getWeekColour in src/application/distribution/get-week-colour.ts; 8 tests against hand-written fakes. Returns the whole banner view (date, isoWeek, colour, isDistributionDay, nextDistribution) so US-004 needs no second use case.

userStories.title: Week colour banner and week lookup on /ausgabe
userStories.description: As a staff member, I want the distribution screen to state today's colour prominently and let me check any other week. Source: tasks/prd-us-03-week-colour.md §US-03.4
acceptanceCriteria: Route /ausgabe shows a large German banner with today's colour, and on a non-distribution day states which colour is next and on which date
The colour is conveyed by text plus colour, never by colour alone
A date picker looks up the colour of any past or future week and shows the ISO week number alongside
The banner is the visually dominant element on the screen
All user-facing strings come from src/i18n/de.ts
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: E2E: week colour banner against a fixed clock
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Playwright spec proving the banner reflects the calendar rule. Source: tasks/prd-us-03-week-colour.md §US-03.5
acceptanceCriteria: The app accepts a test-only fixed clock (env variable or clock override) documented in the spec
Spec asserts the banner text for a Red distribution day, the following week (Blue) and a non-distribution weekday
Spec asserts the lookup control returns the expected colour for a date two years out
npm run test:e2e passes
Typecheck passes
